source: Curated from diggy-tweaks Discord exports + 5 YouTube creator transcripts (fr33thy/lecctron/xilly/lestripez/reknotic) via Gemini 2.5 Flash extraction, hand-reviewed before promotion. v1.8.1 adds per-AC matrix tags on the 12 most-AC-relevant tweaks (kernel mitigations, BCD timer/clock writes, game config FileWrites). Powers Tournament Mode auto-revert/restore on /asta.

tweaks.title: Disable Multi-Plane Overlay (MPO)
tweaks.description: Disables the DWM Multi-Plane Overlay path on dual-monitor + multi-display rigs. MPO routes overlay surfaces through an extra composition layer that frequently stutters when one monitor changes refresh, when HDR toggles, or when capture software hooks the desktop. Most pros run dual-monitor (game + Discord/OBS) — disabling MPO eliminates a stutter source most users never trace.
tweaks.rationale: Microsoft + NVIDIA both acknowledge MPO + multi-monitor causes stutters. The fix has been documented across r/nvidia + Hardware Unboxed since 2022 but is gatekept inside the dual-monitor competitive scene. Single registry value, instantly reversible. Source: NVIDIA driver-team forum responses + Hardware Unboxed multi-monitor stutter coverage.

tweaks.title: TCP: immediate ACK + Nagle off (per-NIC)
tweaks.description: Per-NIC: forces TcpAckFrequency=1 (ACK every packet instead of every 2nd / delayed-ACK) and TCPNoDelay=1 (disables Nagle's algorithm so small packets don't buffer). The two pair — Nagle off without ACK frequency tuning is half the fix. Real win for low-jitter game traffic; controversial only because pre-2010 Windows defaults differed.
tweaks.rationale: Pro-circulated network tweak documented on r/Windows10 + r/CompetitiveApex for years but absent from every mainstream optimizer. Iterates HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters\Interfaces\<NIC GUID> and applies both keys to every interface that has an IP address bound (skips loopback / VPN tunnel adapters). Fully reversible — script accepts -Revert switch.

actions.apply: Get-ChildItem 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters\Interfaces' | ForEach-Object { $k = $_.PsPath; $ip = (Get-ItemProperty -Path $k -Name IPAddress -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).IPAddress; $dhcp = (Get-ItemProperty -Path $k -Name DhcpIPAddress -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).DhcpIPAddress; if (($ip -and $ip -ne '0.0.0.0') -or ($dhcp -and $dhcp -ne '0.0.0.0')) { New-ItemProperty -Path $k -Name TcpAckFrequency -PropertyType DWord -Value 1 -Force | Out-Null; New-ItemProperty -Path $k -Name TCPNoDelay -PropertyType DWord -Value 1 -Force | Out-Null } }; Write-Output 'OK'
actions.revert: Get-ChildItem 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters\Interfaces' | ForEach-Object { $k = $_.PsPath; Remove-ItemProperty -Path $k -Name TcpAckFrequency -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Remove-ItemProperty -Path $k -Name TCPNoDelay -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue }; Write-Output 'OK'

tweaks.title: QoS: DSCP packet tagging for game traffic (gatekept)
tweaks.description: Creates a Group Policy QoS rule (`New-NetQosPolicy`) that tags outbound traffic from FortniteClient-Win64-Shipping.exe + cs2.exe + VALORANT.exe + r5apex.exe with DSCP 46 (Expedited Forwarding). On its own this is just a tag — the routing magic happens at your gateway, where any router that honors DSCP (most consumer routers since ~2018, all enterprise gear) prioritizes EF-tagged packets over your roommate's Netflix. The single most-gatekept network tweak in pro-circle Discords; nobody publishes guides on it.
tweaks.rationale: Pros use this paired with a router-side rule to crush packet loss + jitter spikes during shared-WAN endgame moments. Honest caveat: if your router doesn't honor DSCP, the tag is harmless — packets go out with DSCP=46 in the IP header, your ISP ignores it, no behavior change. We can't auto-config every router brand from the desktop, so the companion guide explains the matching rule for ASUS / Netgear / TP-Link / Ubiquiti / pfSense. Reverts via Remove-NetQosPolicy.

actions.apply: $games = @(@{N='optmaxxing-fortnite';P='FortniteClient-Win64-Shipping.exe'},@{N='optmaxxing-cs2';P='cs2.exe'},@{N='optmaxxing-valorant';P='VALORANT-Win64-Shipping.exe'},@{N='optmaxxing-apex';P='r5apex.exe'}); foreach ($g in $games) { try { Remove-NetQosPolicy -Name $g.N -Confirm:$false -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; New-NetQosPolicy -Name $g.N -AppPathNameMatchCondition $g.P -DSCPAction 46 -NetworkProfile All -Confirm:$false | Out-Null } catch { Write-Output "WARN: $($g.N): $($_.Exception.Message)" } }; Write-Output 'OK'
actions.revert: foreach ($n in @('optmaxxing-fortnite','optmaxxing-cs2','optmaxxing-valorant','optmaxxing-apex')) { try { Remove-NetQosPolicy -Name $n -Confirm:$false -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue } catch {} }; Write-Output 'OK'

tweaks.title: Disable Game DVR & Game Bar
tweaks.description: Stops Windows from running background recording for Game DVR / Xbox Game Bar. Frees CPU + GPU cycles that would otherwise idle-record your gameplay.
tweaks.rationale: Game DVR is the #1 background CPU thief on a stock Windows install. Disabling it reliably recovers 1-3% sustained CPU on top of stutter reduction. Source: diggy-tweaks/cpu, validated by paragontweaks + hone.gg presets.

tweaks.title: Disable Fullscreen Optimizations (global)
tweaks.description: Forces fullscreen-exclusive mode for all DirectX games. Removes the DWM compositor path that adds ~1 frame of input latency on borderless-fullscreen.
tweaks.rationale: FSO routes through DWM and adds presentation latency. Pro players disable this universally. Source: diggy-tweaks/tweaks.

tweaks.title: Disable Mouse Acceleration
tweaks.description: Removes Windows' nonlinear pointer-precision curve. 1:1 mouse-to-cursor mapping, predictable flicks.
tweaks.rationale: Aim-trainer-grade requirement. Mouse acceleration is anti-muscle-memory.

tweaks.title: Visual Effects: Best Performance
tweaks.description: Disables window animations, shadows, and transition fades. Faster context switching, less GPU work for Explorer.
tweaks.rationale: Explorer's animations are pure idle GPU load. Pros run with 'Best Performance' set.

tweaks.title: Disable Network Throttling Index
tweaks.description: Removes Windows' multimedia network rate limit. Frees full pipe to game traffic during gameplay.
tweaks.rationale: From diggy-tweaks/reg-edits: 'completely disables the throttle'. Default value caps multimedia network throughput; gaming benefits from removal.

tweaks.title: System Responsiveness = 0
tweaks.description: Tells the MMCSS scheduler to give 0% CPU reservation to non-multimedia tasks. All headroom goes to games.
tweaks.rationale: From diggy-tweaks/reg-edits. Default 20 reserves a fifth of CPU for other tasks; gaming wants every cycle.

tweaks.title: MMCSS: Games GPU Priority = 8
tweaks.description: Bumps GPU scheduling priority for processes registered as 'Games' with the multimedia scheduler.
tweaks.rationale: Default GPU Priority is 2. Pushing to 8 (max non-realtime) prioritizes game frames over background GPU load.

tweaks.title: Disable Startup Delay
tweaks.description: Removes the 10-second 'wait for startup tasks' delay Windows imposes after login. App launches sooner.
tweaks.rationale: Default StartupDelayInMSec=10000. Zero pushes Windows to launch logon programs immediately.

tweaks.title: Priority Separation: Aggressive Foreground
tweaks.description: Tells the Windows scheduler to bias CPU time toward the focused window. Game gets the lion's share when it has focus.
tweaks.rationale: Win32PrioritySeparation=38 (0x26) decodes to 'Short quanta · Variable · Foreground-boost = High' per the documented bit-layout (foreground:6=High, variable:2=Variable, short:2=Short). Audited 2026-05-07: prior rationale claimed 'long-quanta' which was wrong; the foreground-bias is real, the quanta length is short. Source: diggy-tweaks/cpu.

tweaks.title: Menu Show Delay = 0
tweaks.description: Removes the 400ms hover delay on Windows menus.
tweaks.rationale: Pure latency reduction on shell interactions. Doesn't affect games but feels snappy.

tweaks.title: Disable Windows Telemetry
tweaks.description: Sets the Diagnostic Data policy to Off. Stops the regular telemetry uploads + the heuristics they drive.
tweaks.rationale: Telemetry runs as background processes that wake the CPU on intervals. Disabling reclaims sustained idle. Privacy bonus. **Caveat audited 2026-05-07**: `AllowTelemetry=0` is only honored on Enterprise/Education SKUs; on Home/Pro the effective floor is 1 (Required). Catalog still applies the value (does no harm) but expect partial-effect on consumer SKUs. For Home/Pro, the DiagTrack service-disable + hosts-block is the more effective route.

tweaks.title: Disable Cortana
tweaks.description: Disables the Cortana background search agent and personal assistant features.
tweaks.rationale: Cortana indexes constantly. Disabling stops the SearchHost process from spinning.

tweaks.title: Disable Connected User Experiences (DiagTrack)
tweaks.description: Sets the DiagTrack service Start type to Disabled (4). The biggest ongoing telemetry process.
tweaks.rationale: DiagTrack is the longest-running background tracker on stock Windows. Killing it is the single highest-impact privacy + performance toggle.

tweaks.title: Disable Win11 Widgets
tweaks.description: Stops the Widgets pane (Dsh.exe / WidgetService) from launching at login.
tweaks.rationale: Widgets pulls news + ads + weather over network on a heartbeat. Pure background drain.

tweaks.title: Audio: System Sounds = None
tweaks.description: Sets the system sound scheme to 'No Sounds'. Disables every default Windows event sound.
tweaks.rationale: Each system event (notification, error, USB plug) wakes the audio service. Killing the scheme means audio service stays asleep until games request it.

tweaks.title: Disable HPET (High-Precision Event Timer)
tweaks.description: Sets the hpet driver Start type to Disabled. Forces Windows to fall back to the TSC for timing.
tweaks.rationale: From diggy-tweaks: 'forces Windows to use the TSC instead of the slower HPET. Single most important tweak for 0.1% low stability.' Pairs with bcdedit useplatformclock=no when that lands in Phase 4d.

tweaks.title: Disable Mouse Trails
tweaks.description: Removes the Windows mouse-trail effect entirely.
tweaks.rationale: Trails repaint frames behind the cursor — wastes GPU work. Default is off but some setups enable it accidentally.

tweaks.title: Disable Mouse Pointer Shadow
tweaks.description: Disables the soft shadow under the mouse cursor.
tweaks.rationale: Shadow is composited every frame — small but constant GPU work. Off looks identical on dark themes. Audited 2026-05-07: prior catalog wrote `Cursors\Scheme Source` (cursor scheme origin) which had no effect on shadow; corrected to `Desktop\MouseShadow=0`.

tweaks.title: Hide Win11 Chat / Teams Icon
tweaks.description: Removes the Microsoft Teams chat icon from the Win11 taskbar.
tweaks.rationale: Chat icon spawns msteams.exe lazily; hiding stops the lazy-load probe at logon.

tweaks.title: Disable Activity History
tweaks.description: Stops Windows from logging app + file usage to its Activity History (used for Timeline + cross-device sync).
tweaks.rationale: Activity feed runs as a background process, syncs to Microsoft. Off = privacy + idle CPU recovered.

tweaks.title: BCD: Disable useplatformclock (force TSC)
tweaks.description: Tells the Windows boot loader to ignore HPET/PM-timer and use TSC for the platform clock. Pairs with the HPET-disable registry tweak for full TSC operation.
tweaks.rationale: From diggy-tweaks/cpu: forces Windows to use the TSC instead of the slower HPET. Single most important tweak for 0.1% low stability. Without this, Windows can fall back to HPET even with the driver disabled.

tweaks.title: BCD: Disable Dynamic Tick
tweaks.description: Locks the kernel timer to a constant rate. Removes the dynamic ticking that lets the CPU sleep between interrupts; gains stability at the cost of idle power.
tweaks.rationale: From diggy-tweaks/cpu: disables tickless idle. Smoother frame pacing on desktops where wake-from-idle latency matters more than wattage.

tweaks.title: BCD: TSC Sync Policy = Enhanced
tweaks.description: Forces Windows to use the enhanced TSC synchronization policy across CPU cores. Reduces TSC drift on multi-CCD / multi-die parts (Ryzen, Threadripper).
tweaks.rationale: From diggy-tweaks/cpu. Default policy can let TSC drift between dies on AMD chiplets; Enhanced syncs aggressively. Lower 0.1% lows on Ryzen specifically.

tweaks.title: Disable Power Throttling
tweaks.description: Tells Windows to never throttle background processes for power. Removes the EcoQoS scheduler bias on idle apps that can stall game-process wake-ups.
tweaks.rationale: Cross-validated across fr33thy + lecctron + xilly. Power Throttling can briefly demote game threads when alt-tabbing or when GPU presents stall. Off = consistent thread priority.

tweaks.title: Allow Process-Level Timer Resolution Requests
tweaks.description: Restores Windows 10 1909+ behavior where any process can drop the system timer to 0.5ms via NtSetTimerResolution. Required for TimerResolution.exe + QuickCPU + per-game timer-res hacks to actually take effect.
tweaks.rationale: Win10/11 changed timer resolution to be per-process for power reasons. This reg flag opts back into the legacy global-low-timer-res mode. From reknotic + xilly transcripts; pre-condition for any user-mode timer-res tool.

tweaks.title: Disable Spectre/Meltdown Mitigations (DANGER)
tweaks.description: Turns off Windows' Spectre v2 + Meltdown mitigations. Recovers 3-10% CPU on Intel parts but RE-EXPOSES the original CVEs. Single-user gaming rigs only.
tweaks.rationale: Cross-validated across fr33thy + lecctron + xilly. **Audited 2026-05-07**: `FeatureSettingsOverride=3 + Mask=3` is the original 2018 KB4072698 guidance — covers Spectre v2 + Meltdown ONLY. Does NOT cover MDS / Downfall / L1TF / Retbleed (those need different bit values like Override=72 or 8264 per current MS guidance). Pure performance tradeoff. Apply ONLY on dedicated gaming PCs with no untrusted browsers / mail / VMs. NEVER on shared/work machines. Almost no win on AMD (Meltdown didn't affect AMD); we hard-target Intel.

tweaks.title: Disable USB Selective-Suspend Power Management
tweaks.description: Stops Windows from entering selective-suspend on USB hub devices. Removes the 1-3ms wake stalls that hit when a sleeping USB controller is needed mid-frame.
tweaks.rationale: Cross-validated across fr33thy + xilly. Selective-suspend is the #1 cause of unexplained mouse/keyboard stutter on otherwise-tuned rigs. Off = consistent USB latency.

tweaks.title: Disable HID Power Management
tweaks.description: Companion to the USB power-mgmt tweak — covers HID-class devices (mouse, keyboard, controllers) at the driver layer.
tweaks.rationale: Cross-validated across fr33thy + lecctron. Apply alongside the USB selective-suspend tweak; without this, HID-class devices can still selective-suspend independently.

tweaks.title: Enable Hardware-Accelerated GPU Scheduling (HAGS)
tweaks.description: Turns on HAGS at the driver layer. Lets the GPU manage its own VRAM scheduling, removing the CPU-side scheduling tax (~1-2% CPU win on modern NVIDIA / AMD).
tweaks.rationale: Cross-validated across fr33thy + lestripez transcripts. HAGS is on by default on Win11 22H2+ but off on many Win10 / older Win11 builds. Forces it on. Pairs well with NVIDIA Reflex and DirectStorage.

tweaks.title: Disable Auto-Capture / Clip Recording
tweaks.description: Disables the auto-clip recorder that runs alongside Game DVR. Distinct from the main Game DVR toggle — kills the always-on circular-buffer capture process.
tweaks.rationale: From reknotic. Even with Game DVR off, AppCaptureEnabled can still spin a buffer process that holds GPU resources. Off = full GameDVR shutdown.

tweaks.title: Disable Memory Compression (MMAgent)
tweaks.description: Turns off Windows' compressed-memory background process. Recovers ~1-2% sustained CPU + reduces page-file pressure on systems with adequate RAM (16GB+).
tweaks.rationale: MMAgent's memory-compression saves RAM at the cost of constant CPU work. On 16GB+ rigs, the trade goes the wrong way for gaming. From fr33thy + xilly transcripts. Reversible.

actions.apply: Disable-MMAgent -mc
actions.revert: Enable-MMAgent -mc

tweaks.title: Add 'DT Tournament' Power Plan
tweaks.description: Duplicates the hidden Ultimate Performance scheme and renames it to 'DT Tournament' with a description that flags it as the optimizationmaxxing-tuned profile. Same primitives as Ultimate Performance — full CPU/disk idle throttling off — but BIOS thermal limits + per-core boost stop it from frying the rig. Pairs with manually selecting it from the power dropdown after apply.
tweaks.rationale: Win10/11 ships Ultimate Performance hidden unless on Enterprise/Workstation SKUs. We duplicate, then rename the clone so users can see at a glance which plan came from us. NOTE: 'doesn't fry the PC' is misleading — neither this plan nor any power plan can override BIOS thermal/power limits. The motherboard's PL1/PL2/IccMax + the CPU's thermal trip-point are still active. We just stop Windows from voluntarily under-clocking.

actions.apply: $out = powercfg /duplicatescheme e9a42b02-d5df-448d-aa00-03f14749eb61; if ($out -match '([0-9a-fA-F]{8}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{12})') { $g = $matches[1]; powercfg /changename $g 'DT Tournament' 'optimizationmaxxing-tuned for tournament play. Clones Ultimate Performance + custom name. BIOS thermal limits still active.' | Out-Null }
actions.revert: $plans = powercfg /list | Select-String 'DT Tournament'; foreach ($line in $plans) { if ($line -match '([0-9a-fA-F]{8}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{12})') { powercfg /delete $matches[1] | Out-Null } }

tweaks.title: Block Microsoft Telemetry Endpoints
tweaks.description: Adds 15 Microsoft telemetry hostnames to the hosts file routed to 0.0.0.0. Stops Windows + Office + Edge from phoning home to their analytics + crash-reporter pipelines.
tweaks.rationale: Pairs with the registry telemetry/DiagTrack tweaks. Even with services disabled, some user-mode components fall through to direct HTTPS posts. Hosts-blocking is the belt-and-suspenders. Reversible via the marker block.

actions.apply: $h = "$env:WINDIR\System32\drivers\etc\hosts"; $startMarker = '# === optimizationmaxxing/ms-telemetry begin ==='; $endMarker = '# === optimizationmaxxing/ms-telemetry end ==='; $existing = Get-Content $h -Raw -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; if ($existing -and $existing -match [regex]::Escape($startMarker)) { return }; $hosts = @('vortex.data.microsoft.com','vortex-win.data.microsoft.com','telemetry.microsoft.com','watson.telemetry.microsoft.com','wes.df.telemetry.microsoft.com','services.wes.df.telemetry.microsoft.com','statsfe2.ws.microsoft.com','sqm.df.telemetry.microsoft.com','sqm.telemetry.microsoft.com','v10.events.data.microsoft.com','v20.events.data.microsoft.com','settings-win.data.microsoft.com','oca.telemetry.microsoft.com','reports.wes.df.telemetry.microsoft.com','feedback.windows.com'); $block = @($startMarker) + ($hosts | ForEach-Object { "0.0.0.0  $_" }) + @($endMarker); Add-Content -Path $h -Value ([string]::Join([Environment]::NewLine, $block)) -Encoding ASCII; ipconfig /flushdns | Out-Null
actions.revert: $h = "$env:WINDIR\System32\drivers\etc\hosts"; $startMarker = '# === optimizationmaxxing/ms-telemetry begin ==='; $endMarker = '# === optimizationmaxxing/ms-telemetry end ==='; $content = Get-Content $h -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; if (-not $content) { return }; $out = New-Object System.Collections.Generic.List[string]; $skip = $false; foreach ($line in $content) { if ($line.Trim() -eq $startMarker) { $skip = $true; continue }; if ($line.Trim() -eq $endMarker) { $skip = $false; continue }; if (-not $skip) { $out.Add($line) } }; Set-Content -Path $h -Value $out -Encoding ASCII; ipconfig /flushdns | Out-Null

tweaks.title: Block Windows + Edge Ad / Promo Endpoints
tweaks.description: Adds 10 Windows + Edge ad-network hostnames to the hosts file. Stops Start Menu suggestions, Edge new-tab content feed, and the Lock Screen 'Spotlight' ad rotation.
tweaks.rationale: Spotlight + suggested-apps endpoints make outbound HTTPS calls on every login + screen-wake. Off = quieter network + no surprise tile content. Reversible via the marker block.

actions.apply: $h = "$env:WINDIR\System32\drivers\etc\hosts"; $startMarker = '# === optimizationmaxxing/windows-ads begin ==='; $endMarker = '# === optimizationmaxxing/windows-ads end ==='; $existing = Get-Content $h -Raw -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; if ($existing -and $existing -match [regex]::Escape($startMarker)) { return }; $hosts = @('arc.msn.com','g.msn.com','cdn.atdmt.com','m.adnxs.com','ads.msn.com','ads1.msn.com','rad.msn.com','live.rads.msn.com','msnbot-65-55-108-23.search.msn.com','flex.msn.com'); $block = @($startMarker) + ($hosts | ForEach-Object { "0.0.0.0  $_" }) + @($endMarker); Add-Content -Path $h -Value ([string]::Join([Environment]::NewLine, $block)) -Encoding ASCII; ipconfig /flushdns | Out-Null
actions.revert: $h = "$env:WINDIR\System32\drivers\etc\hosts"; $startMarker = '# === optimizationmaxxing/windows-ads begin ==='; $endMarker = '# === optimizationmaxxing/windows-ads end ==='; $content = Get-Content $h -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; if (-not $content) { return }; $out = New-Object System.Collections.Generic.List[string]; $skip = $false; foreach ($line in $content) { if ($line.Trim() -eq $startMarker) { $skip = $true; continue }; if ($line.Trim() -eq $endMarker) { $skip = $false; continue }; if (-not $skip) { $out.Add($line) } }; Set-Content -Path $h -Value $out -Encoding ASCII; ipconfig /flushdns | Out-Null

tweaks.title: Disable Page Combining (MMAgent)
tweaks.description: Stops Windows from scanning RAM for identical pages and merging them. Pairs with the Disable-MMAgent -mc tweak — together they fully disable Win10/11's RAM-deduplication background work.
tweaks.rationale: Page combining runs every few minutes and walks the entire memory map. On 16GB+ rigs the CPU cost outweighs the RAM savings. Cross-validated by fr33thy + xilly.

actions.apply: Disable-MMAgent -PageCombining
actions.revert: Enable-MMAgent -PageCombining

tweaks.title: Disable SysMain (Superfetch)
tweaks.description: Sets the SysMain service start type to Disabled. Stops Windows from preloading 'predicted' app data — the legacy Superfetch behavior that hurts SSDs more than it helps.
tweaks.rationale: Superfetch's predictive disk reads were a real win on HDDs but waste IO bandwidth on NVMe. Default-on for compatibility; off for any post-2020 build.

tweaks.title: Disable Windows Search Indexer
tweaks.description: Sets the WSearch service to Disabled. Stops the always-on indexing process that scans new files on every save.
tweaks.rationale: Windows Search indexer is one of the heaviest background CPU consumers on a stock install — especially noticeable during gameplay when titles write to AppData. Modern Everything / Voidtools-style search tools cover the same ground without the constant background load.

tweaks.title: BCD: Silent Boot (Skip Boot Logo)
tweaks.description: Tells the Windows boot loader to skip the rotating-dots boot animation. Minor boot-time win + cleaner POST → game-ready handoff.
tweaks.rationale: Saves ~0.5s of boot time and removes the post-bootloader animation that some Quick Resume rigs flicker through. Safe and fully reversible.

tweaks.title: Disable Microsoft Edge Background Mode
tweaks.description: Stops Edge from running background extension hosts and update probes when no Edge windows are open. Frees ~50-200 MB and several background threads on stock Win11.
tweaks.rationale: Edge keeps a hidden 'background mode' process alive even after you close all windows — used for sync, push notifications, and update polling. Off = clean shutdown when you close the last window.

tweaks.title: Disable Discord Auto-Start
tweaks.description: Removes Discord from the login Run key so it doesn't auto-launch + auto-update on boot. Discord still works on-demand.
tweaks.rationale: Discord's startup runs the auto-updater (constant network/disk on slow rigs) plus loads the full Electron shell into memory before you even open a chat. Off = launch when you actually need it.

tweaks.title: Disable Steam Auto-Start
tweaks.description: Removes Steam from the login Run key. Steam still launches when you click a game shortcut or run steam.exe directly — just doesn't auto-boot.
tweaks.rationale: Steam's auto-start spins up the full client + every shader pre-cache + family-view sync immediately on login. Multi-GB of disk read + steady network for 60s after boot. Off = no idle Steam tax.

tweaks.title: Disable Bing Web Search in Start Menu
tweaks.description: Stops the Win10/11 Start menu from sending your typed text to Bing. Search becomes local-only — apps + files + settings, no web roundtrip.
tweaks.rationale: Every keystroke in Start is uploaded to Bing for 'web suggestions'. Off = privacy + zero network roundtrips on every keystroke. Distinct from Cortana service disable — that kills the service, this kills the search-uploads-to-bing pipeline.

tweaks.title: Disable OneDrive Auto-Start
tweaks.description: Removes OneDrive from the Run key so it doesn't auto-launch on login. OneDrive still works on-demand if you launch it manually.
tweaks.rationale: OneDrive's startup probe + initial folder sync wakes the disk + network on every boot. Even with no files synced, the daemon polls Microsoft endpoints every few minutes.

tweaks.title: Disable Win11 Transparency Effects
tweaks.description: Turns off the acrylic/blur compositor effects in the Win11 taskbar, Start menu, and notification center. Removes the per-frame GPU blur passes.
tweaks.rationale: Acrylic blur is composited every frame Explorer is on screen — small but measurable GPU work that runs alongside games on shared-GPU rigs (laptops + iGPU). Off = matte backgrounds, ~0.5% GPU recovered.

tweaks.title: Disable Sticky / Filter / Toggle Keys Shortcuts
tweaks.description: Removes the 'Press Shift 5x' StickyKeys popup, the FilterKeys hold-key shortcut, and the ToggleKeys beeps. The accessibility prompts that pop up mid-game and steal focus.
tweaks.rationale: Every gamer's nightmare — Shift-spam in a melee burst triggers the StickyKeys dialog, which steals window focus and breaks input until you alt-tab. Off = never see it again. Accessibility features themselves stay functional via Settings → Ease of Access if you ever turn them on intentionally.

tweaks.title: Disable Modern Standby (Force S3 Sleep)
tweaks.description: Forces the platform to use the traditional S3 sleep state instead of Modern Standby (S0ix). Sleep actually sleeps — no background wake-from-sleep cycles draining battery / waking GPU.
tweaks.rationale: Modern Standby keeps a 'connected' state running while the screen is off, allowing background sync but causing real wake-cycle drain on desktops that don't need it. S3 is the classic 'really off' sleep. Off = predictable sleep behavior, fans actually stop.

tweaks.title: Disable Win11 Recall (AI Snapshots)
tweaks.description: Stops the Win11 24H2+ Recall feature from taking continuous screenshots + indexing them with the on-device AI model. The well-known privacy / security disaster that Microsoft shipped quietly.
tweaks.rationale: Recall takes screenshots every few seconds and stores them in a local DB. While encrypted, the attack surface (and battery + GPU + disk drain) is real. Off = no screenshot capture, no AI indexing, no Copilot+PC overhead. Required reading before applying: Microsoft's own Recall security disclosures.

tweaks.title: Disable Cloud-Content Promos + 3rd-Party Suggestions
tweaks.description: Disables CloudContent consumer features (movie/app promos, OS feature pushes) and third-party Start-menu suggestions. Reduces network calls + promo surfaces.
tweaks.rationale: Audited 2026-05-07: prior catalog framed this as 'lock-screen Spotlight off' — these two keys target CloudContent / 3rd-party suggestions, NOT the Spotlight image rotation. Lock-screen image rotation is killed by `DisableWindowsSpotlightFeatures=1` + per-user `RotatingLockScreenEnabled=0` (separate tweak owed). Current entry is still useful (kills the Start-menu app suggestions + Office trial promos) but doesn't change the lock-screen image.

tweaks.title: Hide Taskbar Search Box
tweaks.description: Collapses the always-on taskbar search box into a small icon. Reclaims taskbar real estate + removes the always-rendered search field from the compositor's hot path.
tweaks.rationale: The taskbar search box renders on every Explorer redraw + holds keyboard focus when accidentally clicked mid-game. Hidden mode keeps Win+S shortcut working but reclaims the pixels. Win10/11 universal.

tweaks.title: Disable Wake Timers
tweaks.description: Stops scheduled tasks + Windows Update from waking the PC out of sleep. Sleep stays sleep until you press a key.
tweaks.rationale: Wake timers are a hidden cause of 'why did my fans spin up at 3am' — Windows Update + maintenance tasks fire on a wake schedule. Off = predictable idle. Pairs well with Connected Standby disable.

actions.apply: powercfg /setacvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_SLEEP BD3B718A-0680-4D9D-8AB2-E1D2B4AC806D 0; powercfg /setdcvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_SLEEP BD3B718A-0680-4D9D-8AB2-E1D2B4AC806D 0; powercfg /S SCHEME_CURRENT
actions.revert: powercfg /setacvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_SLEEP BD3B718A-0680-4D9D-8AB2-E1D2B4AC806D 1; powercfg /setdcvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_SLEEP BD3B718A-0680-4D9D-8AB2-E1D2B4AC806D 1; powercfg /S SCHEME_CURRENT

tweaks.title: Disable News and Interests Taskbar Feed
tweaks.description: Stops the Win10 22H2+ taskbar weather/news feed from polling Microsoft endpoints + rendering its widget. Reclaims taskbar real estate + a steady network heartbeat.
tweaks.rationale: News and Interests fetches every few minutes and renders a widget on every Explorer paint. Off on most pro rigs. Distinct from Win11 Widgets — this is the Win10 taskbar version.

tweaks.title: Disable Storage Sense Auto-Cleanup
tweaks.description: Stops Storage Sense from auto-emptying Recycle Bin, Downloads, and old OneDrive cloud-only files on a schedule. Lets you control your own cleanup.
tweaks.rationale: Storage Sense runs in the background, scans every drive, and auto-deletes 'unused' files. On rigs with predictable workflow it deletes things you actually wanted. Off = you decide when to clean.

tweaks.title: Disable Win11 Snap Layouts Hover Popup
tweaks.description: Stops the Snap Layouts grid from popping up when you hover over a window's maximize button. Win+Z still works if you want it.
tweaks.rationale: The hover-trigger fires on every accidental near-pass of the maximize button — pure friction during gameplay window-switching. Off = no popup, no flash, hotkey still available.

tweaks.title: Disable Clipboard Cloud Sync
tweaks.description: Stops the Win10/11 cloud clipboard from uploading copied text/images to your Microsoft account for cross-device sync. Local clipboard history (Win+V) still works.
tweaks.rationale: Cloud clipboard uploads literally everything you copy to Microsoft — including passwords, secrets, paste-buffer contents during dev work. Off = local-only clipboard, no upload. Win+V history still functional.

tweaks.title: Disable Hibernation
tweaks.description: Turns off hibernation entirely. Removes hiberfil.sys (frees 4-32GB disk depending on RAM) and the fast-startup overhead that interferes with full-shutdown reboots.
tweaks.rationale: Hibernation matters on laptops only — desktops gain disk space + a clean shutdown path that drivers actually re-init from. Cross-validated by fr33thy + xilly + lestripez.

actions.apply: powercfg /h off
actions.revert: powercfg /h on

tweaks.title: Disable Automatic Windows Driver Updates
tweaks.description: Stops Windows Update from pushing driver updates alongside quality updates. Lets you keep manually-installed drivers (NVIDIA Studio, Realtek custom audio) without WU silently regressing them.
tweaks.rationale: Win Update will silently swap your hand-picked driver for a generic Microsoft one on every quality update — a known cause of audio crackling + VR headset disconnects after a 'cumulative update'. Pro+studio rigs always disable this.

tweaks.title: Disable Windows SmartScreen
tweaks.description: Turns off the SmartScreen reputation-based executable filter. Lets unsigned utilities and custom binaries run without the 'Windows protected your PC' warning + click-through gate.
tweaks.rationale: SmartScreen makes a network call to Microsoft on EVERY new-binary launch. Power users running custom tools, crypto wallets, or self-built utilities pay a 0.5-2s startup tax for the privilege of an upload. Off = no network roundtrip on launch.

tweaks.title: Disable Internet-Zone Download Warning
tweaks.description: Stops File Explorer from blocking downloaded files until you right-click → Unblock. Saves a manual click on every legit download from a non-MOTW source.
tweaks.rationale: The 'this file came from the Internet' MOTW gate is a security feature for office users; for power users running custom tools and patches it's pure friction. Off = no per-file unblock dance. Audited 2026-05-07: prior catalog had value=1 inverted, which preserved zone info; corrected to 2 (strip on save).

tweaks.title: Disable Multi-Plane Overlay (MPO)
tweaks.description: Forces DWM to composite normally instead of using GPU plane overlays. Fixes the well-known multi-monitor stuttering / black-screen flicker that hit a wave of Win10 21H2 + Win11 21H2 users.
tweaks.rationale: MPO is a power optimization that backfires on dual-monitor + variable-refresh setups. The GPU's plane scheduler conflicts with G-Sync/FreeSync. Disabling forces classic compositor path — costs ~1% GPU, restores frame stability.

tweaks.title: Disable Win11 Optimizations for Windowed Games
tweaks.description: Turns off Win11's per-game windowed-mode optimizations (the new Auto-HDR + Variable-Refresh hint layer). Some games stutter when this layer activates mid-frame.
tweaks.rationale: Win11 22H2 added a windowed-game optimization shim that intercepts present calls. Real benefit on some titles, real stutter source on others. Off = predictable presentation.

tweaks.title: Disable Kernel Exception Chain Validation
tweaks.description: Turns off SEHOP-style exception-chain validation in the kernel. Removes a small per-call check that runs on every kernel exception.
tweaks.rationale: From lestripez + reknotic. Real but small CPU win. Has a security cost (slightly easier exploitation if a kernel exploit lands), so we VIP-gate + risk-3 it. Don't enable on machines that run untrusted code.

tweaks.title: BCD: Disable Hyper-V Hypervisor at Boot
tweaks.description: Stops the Hyper-V hypervisor from loading at boot. Removes VBS/HVCI overhead even with Memory Integrity left enabled in Windows Security.
tweaks.rationale: From diggy-tweaks/cpu. Hyper-V's root partition adds VM-exit overhead on every privileged op. Off = bare-metal Windows, ~3-7% CPU recovered on stock Win11.

tweaks.title: NIC: Interrupt Moderation = Disabled
tweaks.description: Disables NIC-level interrupt coalescing on every physical adapter that exposes the property. Each packet generates an immediate interrupt instead of being batched — lowest possible network latency at the cost of CPU.
tweaks.rationale: Source: diggy-tweaks/network. The single highest-impact NIC latency win pros run. Default coalescing batches up to 256 µs of packets to reduce CPU load; disabled = sub-µs interrupt delivery. Tradeoff: more CPU spent in network DPC. Pair with core-parking-disable so a parked core doesn't have to wake first.

actions.apply: $d = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\optmaxxing"; if (-not (Test-Path $d)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $d -Force | Out-Null }; $f = "$d\nic-tweak.log"; $t = Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssK'; Get-NetAdapter -Physical | Where-Object Status -eq 'Up' | ForEach-Object { $n = $_.Name; $p = Get-NetAdapterAdvancedProperty -Name $n -DisplayName 'Interrupt Moderation' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; if (-not $p) { Add-Content $f "$t SKIP $n : Interrupt Moderation not exposed" } else { try { Set-NetAdapterAdvancedProperty -Name $n -DisplayName 'Interrupt Moderation' -DisplayValue 'Disabled' -ErrorAction Stop; Add-Content $f "$t APPLY $n : IntMod -> Disabled" } catch { Add-Content $f "$t FAIL $n : $($_.Exception.Message)" } } }
actions.revert: $d = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\optmaxxing"; if (-not (Test-Path $d)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $d -Force | Out-Null }; $f = "$d\nic-tweak.log"; $t = Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssK'; Get-NetAdapter -Physical | Where-Object Status -eq 'Up' | ForEach-Object { $n = $_.Name; foreach ($v in @('Enabled','Adaptive')) { try { Set-NetAdapterAdvancedProperty -Name $n -DisplayName 'Interrupt Moderation' -DisplayValue $v -ErrorAction Stop; Add-Content $f "$t REVERT $n : IntMod -> $v"; break } catch { } } }

tweaks.title: NIC: Flow Control = Disabled
tweaks.description: Turns off Ethernet pause-frame Flow Control on every up-physical NIC. Switches stop being able to ask the NIC to pause; queue management becomes the host's job.
tweaks.rationale: Source: diggy-tweaks/network. Flow Control's pause frames add jitter on consumer switches that misbehave under load. Pros disable to keep the NIC always-sending. Safe on dedicated gaming switches; can cause drop bursts on enterprise QoS gear — keep on if your switch is managing your VoIP.

actions.apply: $d = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\optmaxxing"; if (-not (Test-Path $d)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $d -Force | Out-Null }; $f = "$d\nic-tweak.log"; $t = Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssK'; Get-NetAdapter -Physical | Where-Object Status -eq 'Up' | ForEach-Object { $n = $_.Name; $p = Get-NetAdapterAdvancedProperty -Name $n -DisplayName 'Flow Control' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; if (-not $p) { Add-Content $f "$t SKIP $n : Flow Control not exposed" } else { try { Set-NetAdapterAdvancedProperty -Name $n -DisplayName 'Flow Control' -DisplayValue 'Disabled' -ErrorAction Stop; Add-Content $f "$t APPLY $n : FlowCtrl -> Disabled" } catch { Add-Content $f "$t FAIL $n : $($_.Exception.Message)" } } }
actions.revert: $d = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\optmaxxing"; if (-not (Test-Path $d)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $d -Force | Out-Null }; $f = "$d\nic-tweak.log"; $t = Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssK'; Get-NetAdapter -Physical | Where-Object Status -eq 'Up' | ForEach-Object { $n = $_.Name; foreach ($v in @('Rx & Tx Enabled','Auto Negotiation','Enabled','Tx Enabled')) { try { Set-NetAdapterAdvancedProperty -Name $n -DisplayName 'Flow Control' -DisplayValue $v -ErrorAction Stop; Add-Content $f "$t REVERT $n : FlowCtrl -> $v"; break } catch { } } }

tweaks.title: NIC: Receive Side Scaling = Enabled
tweaks.description: Force-enables Receive Side Scaling on every up-physical adapter. Spreads incoming-packet processing across CPU cores instead of pinning to core 0.
tweaks.rationale: Source: diggy-tweaks/network. Default-on most modern NICs but vendor drivers sometimes flip off after updates. Idempotent re-enable. Without RSS the network stack is a single-core bottleneck under high pps — multiplayer match servers can saturate it.

actions.apply: $d = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\optmaxxing"; if (-not (Test-Path $d)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $d -Force | Out-Null }; $f = "$d\nic-tweak.log"; $t = Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssK'; Get-NetAdapter -Physical | Where-Object Status -eq 'Up' | ForEach-Object { $n = $_.Name; try { Enable-NetAdapterRss -Name $n -ErrorAction Stop; Add-Content $f "$t APPLY $n : RSS -> Enabled" } catch { Add-Content $f "$t FAIL $n : $($_.Exception.Message)" } }
actions.revert: # Idempotent re-enable; default-on most NICs. If a vendor driver was actively disabling RSS pre-tweak, the user should re-disable manually (rare). Audited 2026-05-07.

tweaks.title: Disable CPU Core Parking (100% min cores)
tweaks.description: Sets the minimum-active-cores power-plan setting to 100% on both AC and DC. Windows scheduler keeps every core spun up — no parking, no wake-from-park latency penalty when a game thread suddenly needs another core.
tweaks.rationale: Source: diggy-tweaks/cpu (Bitsum ParkControl recommendation). The native equivalent of ParkControl's 'Disable parking' without a third-party tool. Wake-from-park = 50-200 µs latency spike when a parked core needs to handle an interrupt or a new thread; disabling = no spike at the cost of slightly higher idle power.

actions.apply: powercfg /setacvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_PROCESSOR 0cc5b647-c1df-4637-891a-dec35c318583 100; powercfg /setdcvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_PROCESSOR 0cc5b647-c1df-4637-891a-dec35c318583 100; powercfg /setactive SCHEME_CURRENT
actions.revert: powercfg /setacvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_PROCESSOR 0cc5b647-c1df-4637-891a-dec35c318583 0; powercfg /setdcvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_PROCESSOR 0cc5b647-c1df-4637-891a-dec35c318583 0; powercfg /setactive SCHEME_CURRENT

tweaks.title: Disable PCIe Link State Power Management (ASPM Off)
tweaks.description: Sets PCI Express ASPM (Active State Power Management) to Off on the current power plan. PCIe lanes stay at full power instead of dropping to L0s/L1 between transfers.
tweaks.rationale: Source: diggy-tweaks/gpu. ASPM transitions add 5-20 µs latency on every PCIe burst. GPU + NVMe + NIC all sit on PCIe — the fix is global. Tradeoff: a few watts of idle power. Worth it on a desktop, defer on laptops.

actions.apply: powercfg /setacvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_PCIEXPRESS ee12f906-d277-404b-b6da-e5fa1a576df5 0; powercfg /setdcvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_PCIEXPRESS ee12f906-d277-404b-b6da-e5fa1a576df5 0; powercfg /setactive SCHEME_CURRENT
actions.revert: powercfg /setacvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_PCIEXPRESS ee12f906-d277-404b-b6da-e5fa1a576df5 1; powercfg /setdcvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_PCIEXPRESS ee12f906-d277-404b-b6da-e5fa1a576df5 2; powercfg /setactive SCHEME_CURRENT

tweaks.title: Disable USB 3 Link Power Management
tweaks.description: Reveals the hidden USB-3 Link Power Management option (Attributes=2) then sets it to Off on both AC and DC. Stops USB-3 ports from dropping to a lower link state between packets — eliminates the wake spike on USB devices like wireless mice + audio interfaces.
tweaks.rationale: Source: diggy-tweaks/device-tweaks. Pairs with NVIDIA HID-power-disable. Polling-rate-1000Hz wireless mice can lose packets when the USB-3 host controller transitions out of U1/U2; off = constant link state, predictable polling. Combine with `process.usb-power-mgmt.disable` for full coverage.

actions.apply: reg add 'HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power\PowerSettings\2a737441-1930-4402-8d77-b2bebba308a3\d4e23884-9b93-478a-9e23-77356611f124' /v Attributes /t REG_DWORD /d 2 /f | Out-Null; powercfg /setacvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT 2a737441-1930-4402-8d77-b2bebba308a3 d4e23884-9b93-478a-9e23-77356611f124 0; powercfg /setdcvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT 2a737441-1930-4402-8d77-b2bebba308a3 d4e23884-9b93-478a-9e23-77356611f124 0; powercfg /setactive SCHEME_CURRENT
actions.revert: powercfg /setacvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT 2a737441-1930-4402-8d77-b2bebba308a3 d4e23884-9b93-478a-9e23-77356611f124 1; powercfg /setdcvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT 2a737441-1930-4402-8d77-b2bebba308a3 d4e23884-9b93-478a-9e23-77356611f124 1; powercfg /setactive SCHEME_CURRENT

tweaks.title: Mouse Class: ThreadPriority = Realtime (31)
tweaks.description: Boosts the mouclass kernel worker thread that drains incoming mouse events to realtime priority. Reduces ISR-to-user-thread propagation jitter.
tweaks.rationale: Source: denis-g/windows10-latency-optimization + djdallmann GamingPCSetup. Not Microsoft-documented at the API level — the value is read by the class driver at boot and used to set its worker-thread priority. Safe: the worker just drains a queue, no userland bypass. Pair with `process.usb-power-mgmt.disable` and `power.usb3.link-power.disable` for full input chain coverage.

tweaks.title: Mouse Class: DataQueueSize = 0x14 (20)
tweaks.description: Caps the mouse data queue at 20 events. Smaller queue = events drain faster + drop sooner under load (preferable to lag-spike accumulation).
tweaks.rationale: Source: legacy MSDN Mouse and Keyboard Driver Entries + community latency guides. Default is 0x64 (100). 20 is the optimization-community consensus — a high-DPI mouse can fill a 100-event queue in <30 ms under heavy motion, which is exactly the jitter we want to drop instead of buffer.

tweaks.title: Keyboard Class: ThreadPriority = Realtime (31)
tweaks.description: Same as the mouse-class realtime boost, applied to kbdclass — keyboard worker thread runs at realtime priority for lower input jitter.
tweaks.rationale: Mirror of `hid.mouse.priority-realtime`. Keyboard input is less jitter-sensitive than mouse (input quanta are 10-30 ms vs mouse's 1-10 ms) but the same mechanism applies and the cost is zero.

tweaks.title: Keyboard Class: DataQueueSize = 0x14 (20)
tweaks.description: Caps the keyboard data queue at 20 events.
tweaks.rationale: Mirror of mouse queue tweak for kbdclass.

tweaks.title: Disable HVCI / Memory Integrity (VBS off)
tweaks.description: Disables Hypervisor-Enforced Code Integrity at the registry-policy level. Companion to `bcd.hypervisorlaunchtype.off` — both are needed for VBS to fully cost 0%. Settings → Device security → Core isolation → Memory Integrity must also be flipped manually if it's on (we can't toggle it programmatically without trust-store risk).
tweaks.rationale: Microsoft Learn: HVCI is the primary VBS perf cost. Disabling can recover 5-15% in CPU-bound games (28% on Cyberpunk in some tests). **Anti-cheat caveat**: Riot Vanguard, FACEIT, BattlEye on some titles refuse to run without VBS / Secure Boot / TPM. Verify your game list before applying. Pair with `bcd.hypervisorlaunchtype.off` and reboot for full effect; check the VBS Status card on /diagnostics afterward.

tweaks.title: Fortnite: launch with High CPU priority (IFEO)
tweaks.description: Image File Execution Options entry that launches FortniteClient-Win64-Shipping.exe with CPU Priority Class = High (3). Persistent across launches — set once, applies forever.
tweaks.rationale: Source: Bitsum + Microsoft IFEO docs. CpuPriorityClass=3 (High) is the safe ceiling — Realtime (4) starves keyboard/mouse input threads and can hard-freeze the rig. High biases the scheduler toward the game without input starvation. Note: the IFEO `Affinity` value people quote is FOLKLORE — only `CpuPriorityClass` actually works.

tweaks.title: Valorant: launch with High CPU priority (IFEO)
tweaks.description: IFEO entry for VALORANT-Win64-Shipping.exe → CpuPriorityClass = High.
tweaks.rationale: See `process.fortnite.priority-high` for the rationale + caveat about IFEO Affinity being folklore.

tweaks.title: CS2: launch with High CPU priority (IFEO)
tweaks.description: IFEO entry for cs2.exe → CpuPriorityClass = High.
tweaks.rationale: See `process.fortnite.priority-high`.

tweaks.title: Disable Windows Error Reporting Service
tweaks.description: Sets WerSvc Start = 4 (Disabled). Stops the WER background process from waking on every crash + uploading dumps.
tweaks.rationale: Source: djdallmann + Raphire/Win11Debloat. Pure telemetry / crash-upload service; disabling has zero gameplay impact. Crash dialogs still appear (those are user-mode); only the upload pipeline goes quiet.

tweaks.title: Disable Downloaded Maps Manager
tweaks.description: Sets MapsBroker Start = 4. Stops the offline-maps service that runs on a schedule for the Windows Maps app.
tweaks.rationale: If you don't use Windows Maps app, this is pure dead weight. Permanent disable.

tweaks.title: Disable Retail Demo
tweaks.description: Sets RetailDemo Start = 4. The Best Buy / store-demo mode service.
tweaks.rationale: Zero use to a real user. Permanent disable.

tweaks.title: Disable Fax service
tweaks.description: Sets Fax Start = 4. The Windows fax-machine service.
tweaks.rationale: Permanent disable for any rig that's not literally a fax appliance.

tweaks.title: Disable Windows Media Player Network Sharing
tweaks.description: Sets WMPNetworkSvc Start = 4. The DLNA-share service for WMP.
tweaks.rationale: Permanent disable unless you DLNA-share your library.

tweaks.title: Disable Geolocation service
tweaks.description: Sets lfsvc Start = 4. Stops the Windows Location Service from pinging WiFi-positioning + GPS.
tweaks.rationale: Privacy + idle-CPU win. Disables location for any app that uses Win32 location API. If you actually use location-aware apps (Weather widget, Find My Device), keep on.

tweaks.title: Disable Smart Card services
tweaks.description: Disables SCardSvr + ScDeviceEnum + SCPolicySvc. The Windows smart-card stack.
tweaks.rationale: Used for federal/CAC card login + some enterprise auth. Zero use to home gaming rigs. Permanent disable.

tweaks.title: Disable Windows Biometric Service
tweaks.description: Sets WbioSrvc Start = 4. The Hello-fingerprint / face / iris auth service.
tweaks.rationale: Disable on rigs without biometric hardware OR rigs that don't use Hello.

tweaks.title: Disable Touch Keyboard / Tablet Input
tweaks.description: Sets TabletInputService Start = 4. The on-screen-keyboard + pen-input service.
tweaks.rationale: Pure desktop dead weight. Touch / pen rigs should keep on. `formFactor: desktop` recommended.

tweaks.title: Disable 13 Microsoft telemetry / promo scheduled tasks
tweaks.description: Disables a curated batch of Microsoft\Windows scheduled tasks that exist purely for telemetry upload, compatibility-appraiser, customer-experience-improvement program, and store-app promo. Reverts to enable.
tweaks.rationale: Source: Raphire/Win11Debloat + djdallmann. These tasks wake the system on a schedule even when telemetry services are disabled, because the scheduler runs them as one-shots. Disabling closes the back-door. Note: some get re-enabled after major Windows feature updates; re-apply post-update.

actions.apply: $tasks = @('\Microsoft\Windows\Application Experience\Microsoft Compatibility Appraiser','\Microsoft\Windows\Application Experience\ProgramDataUpdater','\Microsoft\Windows\Application Experience\StartupAppTask','\Microsoft\Windows\Application Experience\PcaPatchDbTask','\Microsoft\Windows\Customer Experience Improvement Program\Consolidator','\Microsoft\Windows\Customer Experience Improvement Program\KernelCeipTask','\Microsoft\Windows\Customer Experience Improvement Program\UsbCeip','\Microsoft\Windows\Autochk\Proxy','\Microsoft\Windows\DiskDiagnostic\Microsoft-Windows-DiskDiagnosticDataCollector','\Microsoft\Windows\Maps\MapsToastTask','\Microsoft\Windows\Maps\MapsUpdateTask','\Microsoft\Windows\Feedback\Siuf\DmClient','\Microsoft\Windows\Feedback\Siuf\DmClientOnScenarioDownload','\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Error Reporting\QueueReporting'); foreach ($p in $tasks) { $tn = ($p -split '\\')[-1]; $tp = $p.Substring(0, $p.Length - $tn.Length); try { Disable-ScheduledTask -TaskPath $tp -TaskName $tn -ErrorAction Stop | Out-Null } catch { } }
actions.revert: $tasks = @('\Microsoft\Windows\Application Experience\Microsoft Compatibility Appraiser','\Microsoft\Windows\Application Experience\ProgramDataUpdater','\Microsoft\Windows\Application Experience\StartupAppTask','\Microsoft\Windows\Application Experience\PcaPatchDbTask','\Microsoft\Windows\Customer Experience Improvement Program\Consolidator','\Microsoft\Windows\Customer Experience Improvement Program\KernelCeipTask','\Microsoft\Windows\Customer Experience Improvement Program\UsbCeip','\Microsoft\Windows\Autochk\Proxy','\Microsoft\Windows\DiskDiagnostic\Microsoft-Windows-DiskDiagnosticDataCollector','\Microsoft\Windows\Maps\MapsToastTask','\Microsoft\Windows\Maps\MapsUpdateTask','\Microsoft\Windows\Feedback\Siuf\DmClient','\Microsoft\Windows\Feedback\Siuf\DmClientOnScenarioDownload','\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Error Reporting\QueueReporting'); foreach ($p in $tasks) { $tn = ($p -split '\\')[-1]; $tp = $p.Substring(0, $p.Length - $tn.Length); try { Enable-ScheduledTask -TaskPath $tp -TaskName $tn -ErrorAction Stop | Out-Null } catch { } }

tweaks.title: Enable MSI mode on GPU + NIC + Audio (safe-class only)
tweaks.description: Enumerates Display + Net + AudioEndpoint PCI devices and sets MSISupported=1 under each device's Interrupt Management key. Hard-excludes storage controllers + USB host controllers (documented breakage). Logs each apply / fail to %LOCALAPPDATA%\optmaxxing\msi-mode.log.
tweaks.rationale: Source: Microsoft Learn (MSI registry path) + community validation (guru3D, GoInterruptPolicy). Message-Signaled Interrupts pin device IRQs to the device's own MSI vector instead of sharing a line-based IRQ — measurable input + DPC-latency win on GPU + NIC. **Hard exclusions**: StorAHCI / NVMe / xHCI USB host controllers (Dell KB 000125194 documents 100% disk utilization storms; overclock.net 'Muh deadzone' thread documents xHCI input lag with MSI on). Reboot required.

actions.apply: $d = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\optmaxxing"; if (-not (Test-Path $d)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $d -Force | Out-Null }; $f = "$d\msi-mode.log"; $t = Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssK'; $classes = @('Display','Net','AudioEndpoint'); foreach ($c in $classes) { Get-PnpDevice -Class $c -PresentOnly -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq 'OK' -and $_.InstanceId -like 'PCI\\*' } | ForEach-Object { $regPath = "HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Enum\$($_.InstanceId)\Device Parameters\Interrupt Management\MessageSignaledInterruptProperties"; if (-not (Test-Path $regPath)) { New-Item -Path $regPath -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Out-Null }; try { Set-ItemProperty -Path $regPath -Name MSISupported -Value 1 -Type DWord -ErrorAction Stop; Add-Content $f "$t APPLY $($_.FriendlyName) ($c)" } catch { Add-Content $f "$t FAIL $($_.FriendlyName) ($c) : $($_.Exception.Message)" } } }
actions.revert: $d = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\optmaxxing"; if (-not (Test-Path $d)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $d -Force | Out-Null }; $f = "$d\msi-mode.log"; $t = Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssK'; $classes = @('Display','Net','AudioEndpoint'); foreach ($c in $classes) { Get-PnpDevice -Class $c -PresentOnly -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.Status -eq 'OK' -and $_.InstanceId -like 'PCI\\*' } | ForEach-Object { $regPath = "HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Enum\$($_.InstanceId)\Device Parameters\Interrupt Management\MessageSignaledInterruptProperties"; if (Test-Path $regPath) { try { Set-ItemProperty -Path $regPath -Name MSISupported -Value 0 -Type DWord -ErrorAction Stop; Add-Content $f "$t REVERT $($_.FriendlyName) ($c)" } catch { Add-Content $f "$t REVERT-FAIL $($_.FriendlyName) ($c) : $($_.Exception.Message)" } } } }

tweaks.title: NIC: Disable Receive Segment Coalescing (RSC) — fiber
tweaks.description: Disables Receive Segment Coalescing on every up-physical NIC. RSC merges incoming TCP segments into larger ones at the NIC level to reduce CPU interrupt load — but on a high-end fiber connection, modern CPUs can handle the per-segment work and benefit from lower-latency packet delivery vs the coalescing batch delay.
tweaks.rationale: Source: Microsoft Learn `Set-NetAdapterRsc` cmdlet docs + competitive-FPS community testing. RSC was designed for 1Gbps NICs feeding slow CPUs — modern 2.5/10Gbps + Ryzen/Raptor Lake doesn't need the help. Disabling = packets delivered as the NIC receives them, lower latency at slightly higher CPU. Pairs with `net.nic.interrupt-moderation.disable`. Logs to `%LOCALAPPDATA%\optmaxxing\nic-tweak.log`.

actions.apply: $d = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\optmaxxing"; if (-not (Test-Path $d)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $d -Force | Out-Null }; $f = "$d\nic-tweak.log"; $t = Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssK'; Get-NetAdapter -Physical | Where-Object Status -eq 'Up' | ForEach-Object { $n = $_.Name; try { Disable-NetAdapterRsc -Name $n -ErrorAction Stop; Add-Content $f "$t APPLY $n : RSC -> Disabled" } catch { Add-Content $f "$t FAIL $n : $($_.Exception.Message)" } }
actions.revert: $d = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\optmaxxing"; if (-not (Test-Path $d)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $d -Force | Out-Null }; $f = "$d\nic-tweak.log"; $t = Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssK'; Get-NetAdapter -Physical | Where-Object Status -eq 'Up' | ForEach-Object { $n = $_.Name; try { Enable-NetAdapterRsc -Name $n -ErrorAction Stop; Add-Content $f "$t REVERT $n : RSC -> Enabled" } catch { } }

tweaks.title: NIC: Disable TCP/IPv4 Checksum Offload
tweaks.description: Disables hardware checksum offloading for IPv4 + TCP on every up-physical NIC. The CPU computes packet checksums instead of the NIC.
tweaks.rationale: Source: Microsoft Learn `Set-NetAdapterChecksumOffload` + community testing. On a fast modern CPU (Ryzen 5xxx+, Raptor Lake), CPU-computed checksums add < 1% sustained CPU but eliminate one source of variable latency in the NIC's packet-processing pipeline — pros report more consistent frametimes after disable. Tradeoff: slightly higher CPU during heavy network load. Skip on laptops / mid-range systems where the offload is genuinely saving cycles.

actions.apply: $d = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\optmaxxing"; if (-not (Test-Path $d)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $d -Force | Out-Null }; $f = "$d\nic-tweak.log"; $t = Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssK'; Get-NetAdapter -Physical | Where-Object Status -eq 'Up' | ForEach-Object { $n = $_.Name; try { Disable-NetAdapterChecksumOffload -Name $n -ErrorAction Stop; Add-Content $f "$t APPLY $n : ChecksumOffload -> Disabled" } catch { Add-Content $f "$t FAIL $n : $($_.Exception.Message)" } }
actions.revert: $d = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\optmaxxing"; if (-not (Test-Path $d)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $d -Force | Out-Null }; $f = "$d\nic-tweak.log"; $t = Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssK'; Get-NetAdapter -Physical | Where-Object Status -eq 'Up' | ForEach-Object { $n = $_.Name; try { Enable-NetAdapterChecksumOffload -Name $n -ErrorAction Stop; Add-Content $f "$t REVERT $n : ChecksumOffload -> Enabled" } catch { } }

tweaks.title: NIC: Disable Large Send Offload (LSO)
tweaks.description: Disables Large Send Offload on every up-physical NIC. LSO lets the NIC fragment large outbound TCP segments — disabling it makes the CPU do that work, eliminating one source of NIC-side queuing delay.
tweaks.rationale: LSO is a throughput optimization (helps file transfers + bulk uploads); for game traffic which is usually small UDP packets it does nothing useful, and on some Killer/Realtek consumer NICs the LSO logic introduces measurable latency variance. Tradeoff: slower sustained large uploads. Apply on dedicated gaming rigs; skip on a workstation that's also pulling video uploads.

actions.apply: $d = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\optmaxxing"; if (-not (Test-Path $d)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $d -Force | Out-Null }; $f = "$d\nic-tweak.log"; $t = Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssK'; Get-NetAdapter -Physical | Where-Object Status -eq 'Up' | ForEach-Object { $n = $_.Name; try { Disable-NetAdapterLso -Name $n -ErrorAction Stop; Add-Content $f "$t APPLY $n : LSO -> Disabled" } catch { Add-Content $f "$t FAIL $n : $($_.Exception.Message)" } }
actions.revert: $d = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\optmaxxing"; if (-not (Test-Path $d)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $d -Force | Out-Null }; $f = "$d\nic-tweak.log"; $t = Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssK'; Get-NetAdapter -Physical | Where-Object Status -eq 'Up' | ForEach-Object { $n = $_.Name; try { Enable-NetAdapterLso -Name $n -ErrorAction Stop; Add-Content $f "$t REVERT $n : LSO -> Enabled" } catch { } }

tweaks.title: CS2: competitive autoexec.cfg
tweaks.description: Writes a converged-pro autoexec.cfg to Steam\steamapps\common\Counter-Strike Global Offensive\game\csgo\cfg\. Locks raw input on, m_customaccel off, 128-tick netcode (cl_cmdrate/cl_updaterate 128, cl_interp 0, cl_interp_ratio 1, lag-comp on), fps_max 0, mat_queue_mode 2 (multithreaded), snd_mixahead 0.025 for tighter audio cue timing.
tweaks.rationale: Source: ZywOo / s1mple / NiKo / m0nesy converged on these values circa 2024-2026. The 128-tick + cl_interp 0 + cl_interp_ratio 1 trio is what every IEM Cologne config exports. mat_queue_mode 2 forces multithreaded rendering — Source 2 lobbies fail to multi-thread on a few rigs without the explicit forcing. fps_max 0 lets the GPU run uncapped (use NVCP frame cap instead, per the Reflex whitepaper). Reverts cleanly via the FileWrite snapshot store.

tweaks.title: Apex: competitive videoconfig.txt
tweaks.description: Writes a stripped videoconfig.txt to %USERPROFILE%\Saved Games\Respawn\Apex\local\. Disables gibs, ragdoll collision, particle multipliers, film grain, dynamic vertical sync, cascade shadow maps, dynamic super-sampling. **After applying, manually `attrib +R` the file** so the game can't overwrite — Apex rewrites videoconfig on launch otherwise.
tweaks.rationale: Source: Aceu / sweet / ImperialHal config posts, cross-validated against r/CompetitiveApex pinned threads. Visual effects that don't help target identification cost ~5-12% framerate in dense fights. Read-only enforcement is mandatory because Apex's renderer rewrites videoconfig.txt at launch with whatever it thinks the GPU can handle.

tweaks.title: Fortnite: competitive Engine.ini
tweaks.description: Writes a competitive-baseline Engine.ini to %LOCALAPPDATA%\FortniteGame\Saved\Config\WindowsClient\. Forces post-processing / shadow / view-distance / effects / foliage scalability to 0, motion blur off, anti-aliasing off, anisotropy off, mesh LOD bias maxed (so distant geometry stays simple). Logs muted. Snapshot-backed revert restores whatever Engine.ini was there before.
tweaks.rationale: Source: Bugha + Mongraal + Clix published configs converge on these values. The same Engine.ini tweaks Eklipse / OpusClip / etc. all ship as 'pro config'. Free 6-12% FPS on most rigs in dense endgame storms — these values just disable rendering work the player can't notice anyway. Reverts cleanly via the FileWrite snapshot store. Lives in the user-profile path, no admin needed.

tweaks.title: Fortnite: competitive GameUserSettings.ini
tweaks.description: Pairs with Engine.ini. Writes %LOCALAPPDATA%\FortniteGame\Saved\Config\WindowsClient\GameUserSettings.ini with FrameRateLimit=240, bUseMaxFrameRate=True, bUseVSync=False, bMotionBlur=False, bShowGrass=False, bUseHDRDisplayOutput=False, fullscreen-windowed mode (FullscreenMode=0 — Fortnite's lowest-input-lag mode since fullscreen-exclusive was removed in Ch5), 1920x1080. Snapshot-backed revert restores the prior file byte-for-byte.
tweaks.rationale: Engine.ini sets render scalability; GameUserSettings.ini sets the things you can't touch from Engine.ini — frame cap, VSync, fullscreen mode, resolution. Locks in the Peterbot/Clix/Bugha consensus. NOTE: Fortnite rewrites GameUserSettings.ini on every launch with whatever the in-game Settings panel last had. To make this stick across launches, mark the file read-only after applying — or just re-apply after any session where you opened in-game Settings. We don't auto-set read-only because that breaks legitimate in-game tweaking.
